title: Union Types
description: Allow variables to be one of multiple types.

slides.title: When One Type Isn't Enough 🤷‍♂️
slides.content:
Sometimes a value could legitimately be more than one type depending on the situation.

For example, an API might return a successfully parsed `number` ID, but if it fails, it might return a `string` saying 'Error'.

slides.title: The Union Operator
slides.content: TypeScript allows you to create a **Union Type** using the pipe `|` character. It acts as an 'OR' operator for types.

quizData.question: Which character creates a Union Type?
quizData.explanation: The 'pipe' `|` character unites types, meaning 'this type OR that type'.

slides.title: Arrays of Multiple Types 📚
slides.content: You can use parentheses with Unions to easily create arrays that are allowed to hold a mix of specific types!

slides.title: Union Arrays
slides.content: This array permits strings AND numbers natively.
